title: The One Where Ross Moves In

lines.text: Central Perk, Chandler and Joey are there. Joey is looking at a National Geographic and giggling.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Are you looking at naked tribe's women?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, look. (Shows him the magazine.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's a pig.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know, I know, but look at the knobs on her.
Ross enters and his hair is a mess.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey! (Joey quickly hides the magazine under the couch.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Emily's cousin kicked me out!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, when you're subletting an apartment from your wife's cousin and then you get a divorce, sometimes the cousin suddenly wants his apartment back.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How can he do that? Didn't you sign a lease?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Who needs a lease when it's family!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, you can stay with us! We'll take care of ya!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, yeah! Absolutely! Anything you need man! But you have to promise me the second you are feeling better so that we can make fun of your hair!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You got it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thanks you guys, I really appreciate this. All right, I'm gonna get packing again. Man, I've been moving around so much I'm beginning to feel like a nomad.
Joey starts giggling.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: He thought you said gonad.
Joey busts out laughing.
Opening Credits
Monica's restaurant, she is getting inspected by the health department, Phoebe is watching.

lines.speaker: Health Inspector
lines.text: Wow, Monica, if every restaurant is as clean as yours, I'd have a tough time making a living.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, Larry.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, do health inspectors work on commission?

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: No, bribes.
Phoebe laughs.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's okay to laugh right?

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Yeah, I was just kidding.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. (She laughs harder.)

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: I'll check the kitchen floors.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, knock yourself out, Larry.
He goes into the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yum-my!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Larry?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yeah! I'd let him check out my kitchen floors.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: (entering) A 98. I deducted 2 points because you are not wearing your chef's hat, and that is a Section 5 violation.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uh, look, Larry honey, umm, I wrote the book on Section 5 and I know that you don't have to wear your hat unless you're in the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: And where is your hat?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's in the kitchen, I'll go get it.
She heads for the kitchen door and just after she goes through the door...

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Ahh that's the 2 points.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, you should really read that book you wrote. (To Larry) Wow! You saw the hat in the kitchen and knew that she'd have to go in there hatless to get it. You can have your own health inspector detective show!

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Oh, I don't know about that.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, but then I can be you sidekick Vunda.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Maybe uh, Vunda could give me her number and I can ask her to dinner sometime.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, she would love that! Y'know, 'cause you know all the clean places to eat.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: I-I'll call ya.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.
Larry goes to leave but heads the wrong way and makes a quick sidestep to go out the right door.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: He's so funny! (She imitates what he just did.)
Central Perk, Gunther is serving Monica and Rachel.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you. (To Monica) Mon?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hmm?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: How's Ross doing? Y'know since all the Emily stuff.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: He's not great umm, but he's dealing with it. Oh wait a minute, you're not gonna try...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, honey, please, no, I can't get started with all that Ross stuff again. I mean, he's gonna screwed up for a looong time. And besides y'know, I don't, I don't go for guys right after they get divorced.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Right, you only go for them 5 minutes before they get married.
Danny enters.

lines.speaker: Danny
lines.text: (To Gunther) Two pounds of Moca Java please.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (To Monica) Danny. Are you guys ever gonna go out again?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know! He hasn't called me since that one time when we went out. I see him in the hallway, we flirt, I'm all ha-ha-ha-ha, and nothing.

lines.speaker: Danny
lines.text: (To them) Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi Danny! (Notices his box of liquor he's carrying.) Wow! Thirsty huh?

lines.speaker: Danny
lines.text: Uh, actually, actually, I'm having a party at my place on Saturday, it's sort of a house warming kind of thing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ohh, fun!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, great!

lines.speaker: Danny
lines.text: Yeah, I'm really looking forward to it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Danny
lines.text: Okay, see ya. (Heads out.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, I guess we won't be warming his house.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (laughs) Okay. All right, I see what he's doing! He's not asking me out, because he wants me to ask him out.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And you're not gonna do that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That's right! 'Cause that would give him the control! So now he's all ooh, coming up with this whole I've got a party thing y'know, trying to get me to hint around for an invitation. Blew up in his face, didn't it?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So-so there is no party.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, there's a party. There's a party. But the power, that is still up for grabs. You follow me?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I think so. Se, he-he's not inviting you to his party because he likes you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Exactly.
Chandler and Joey's, they're entering to find boxes strewn about the apartment.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ross?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering from the bathroom) Hey roomies!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Love what you've done with the place.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, yeah I know, I know, it's a lot of boxes, but again I really appreciate you guys letting me stay here.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Not a problem. And listen, hey! Since you're gonna be here for a while, why don't-I was thinking we uh, put your name on the answering machine.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I uh, hope you don't mind, I kinda uh, jazzed it up a little. Check this out. (He plays the greeting, and We Will Rock You starts to play and Ross's voice comes over it.) We will, we will, call you back!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, all right!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Pretty cool, huh?
They both laugh as Ross heads back to the bathroom.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (To Chandler) You're fake laughing too, right?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, the tears are real.
A restaurant, Phoebe and Larry are having dinner.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: You look beautiful this evening.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (smiles) Show me the badge again.
He looks around and flashes her his badge and she laughs.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Shiny.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Oh, will you mind if I wash up? Because I came straight from work and who knows where these babies (Holds up his hands) have been.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (laughing) You are just nonstop!
He goes to the bathroom and Phoebe puts some pepper and salt on her food. With the salt she takes a bit and throws it over her left shoulder as she faces us.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: (coming back) We're outta here!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why?!

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Just walking past the kitchen I saw 10 violations! I'm shutting this place down!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (awed) You have the power to do that?

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: This does. (Shows her his badge.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (excited) Shut it down.
Chandler and Joey's, Chandler is entering. As he closes the door, Joey pokes his head up from a box enclosure built using the 2 chairs.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nothing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You built a fort didn't ya?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (smiles) Kinda.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (notices something) Oh my God, the air purifier! Ross's air purifier! All I heard through 4 years of college was (makes a humming noise.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Dude, you should've gone out once and a while.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I hate this thing!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on, Chandler, Ross is our friend. He needs us right now, so why don't you be a grown up and come and watch some TV in the fort!
Monica and Rachel's, Rachel is opening the door.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, hi Danny.

lines.speaker: Danny
lines.text: Hey guys, I just uh, wanted to invite you to the party tomorrow night.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, thanks! We'll try to stop by.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh, actually, I think I'm gonna be busy.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You are?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah! Remember I got that uh, gala.

lines.speaker: Danny
lines.text: Yeah, what's the gala for?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's a uh, regatta gala.

lines.speaker: Danny
lines.text: Really! You-you sail?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No-no, but I support it.

lines.speaker: Danny
lines.text: Okay, (To Monica) hope I see you tomorrow night.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Danny
lines.text: Take care. (Leaves.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. (Closes the door.) Walked right into that one didn't he?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What one? You wanted him to invite you to the party and he did it!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, but he waited until the last minute! So if I said yes, he would know I had nothing better to do than wait around for an invitation to his stupid party. I said, "No!" Which puts me right back in the driver seat.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Great. So the ball is in his court?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ball? There is no ball.
Chandler and Joey's and Ross's, Ross is working on his computer and Joey is making a lot of noise.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (glaring at him) Joey, please! (Motions to his computer.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sorry.
Joey starts playing with a toy alligator and has it attack him.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ahhhhhhhhhh...

Notices Ross looking at him and stops. Ross gives him his 'quiet down' maneuver. Okay, this may take a while to explain, so center this on you screen and place your hands about a foot apart with your fingers together and pointing straight up. Now take you fingers and point them at the other hand and making a 90-degree angle with each of your hands and the first knuckle counting up from the wrist. Now take your right hand, no your other right (that was for the dyslexics), and lower it a couple of inches, so that the fingers are pointing at your wrist. Now take your arms and keeping the elbows bent and your hands in front of you spread out your arms, kinda like making a bird's wing. Now hunch your shoulders over and move you hands up and down as if you are trying to tell some one to turn it down. That's Ross 'quiet down' maneuver. Well, there is an accompanying face, but I don't want to try and describe it as well.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering) Hello children!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! Wanna play some foosball? Please?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. (Starts to head for where the foosball table usually is.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-no, no! We have to move the table into my room, yeah! 'Cause of all the boxes. Come on!
They go into his bedroom.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, I have one question. What is the deal with this? (Imitates Ross's 'quiet down' maneuver, but does move his hands up and down he just flaps his hands as if he's waving good-bye.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Bye-bye little puppet Joey hand?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, the quiet down thing!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You mean this. (Does the maneuver perfectly.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Um-hmm! Look, I-I-I don't know how much more of this I can take! Did you know he taped over my Baywatch tape with some show about bugs! My God! What if that had been porn?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (gasps) All right look, y'know, this maybe tough but come on, this is Ross! I survived college with him!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, I guess I can hold out a little longer. Let's have a game.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.
They start playing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No-no-no-no!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: YES!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) Uh fellas, (Does the maneuver and gives them a double thumbs up, which Chandler returns as he closes the door.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, so he's out of here.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Um-hmm.
Commercial Break
Monica and Rachel's, Monica, Rachel, and Phoebe are there.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You guys got anything to eat? I just went down to Johnos for some chicken and it was closed!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I took Larry there to eat but it was all violated. So we shut it down!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Pheebs, if this guy keeps closing down all of our favorite places, where are we gonna eat?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't know, clean places?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Umm, yum!
There's a knock on the door and Monica answers it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (looking through the peephole) It's Danny.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Don't let him in! I'm supposed to be at a regatta gala.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Danny) We'll be right there! (To Rachel) Can't you just say it starts later?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? What kind of a regatta gala starts at night?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: The fake kind!
She opens the door and Rachel hides behind it.

lines.speaker: Danny
lines.text: Hey, hi, I need a ladle. You got a ladle?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We have a ladle. (Gives him one.)

lines.speaker: Danny
lines.text: Thanks, see you at the party.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, great!
He leaves and she closes the door.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, guys, you know what Larry would say? He would say, "See you ladle." (Laughs.)
Central Perk, Chandler, Joey, and Ross are there. Chandler and Joey are looking through the paper.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I-I-I'm done with this. You want anything Ross? Sports? International? Apartment listings?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'll take sports.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Mine! (He grabs it.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right. Uhh, international.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh that's mine too! (Grabs it and Ross looks at him.) I'm Italian!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I guess I can check out those apartment listings, even though there's never anything in here.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Not even on page 7?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (looks) Oh yeah! You're-hey, you're right! Here's an affordable place, (reading ad) two bedroom, close to work, ooh, it's available in five weeks!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What about that circled one?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, I-I don't know, it's kind of expensive for a studio.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But it's available now! Isn't it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, it is.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, let's go look at it! (They both jump up.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, let's go.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: There we go!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh-oh-ooh, hey guys, I was wondering if you guys would uh, maybe chip in on some new air filters for the air purifier? I mean after all, we all are using it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Let's go quicker.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah!
The apartment in the listing, the guys are checking it out. There's one problem though, it's roughly the size of this computer screen. As they enter Joey lets out a whistle.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God! (Looking around, which doesn't take him long.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, well look at this kitchen, slash bathroom. Well that's great! Y'know so you can cook while in the tub.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Somebody was using his head. Hey, let's check out the rest of the place.
They don't move, just look all around them.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I think this is it. I don't know, maybe we should keep looking.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But hey, Ross, this place is available now!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, you don't want to be stuck with us for the next five weeks.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.
He looks at them.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (To Joey) So, you-you think I should go ahead and take this place?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, it's perfect!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (To Chandler) How about you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's a kitchen slash bathroom.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right, I see what you guys are saying. I'll uh, I'll go downstairs and fill out an application.
He exits.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We are bad people.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: He knew we were trying to get rid of him. He knew! (Pause.) You think we could get a bathtub in our kitchen?
Central Perk, Phoebe is there waiting for Larry.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: (entering) Hey, ready for dinner?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, absolutely!

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Great! How about you wanted to go the Italian place down on Bleaker Street right?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, I love that place! (Thinks about it.) So, no.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: How about Mama Lisettie's?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Enh. Sure!

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: (notices something) I wonder how long that milk (on the counter) has been setting out.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, no-no, this place is totally healthy! That-this milk is mine. I bought this today, 'cause I was thirsty for milk, y'know. (She takes a swig of it, but has to turn away from him as she makes a face to show that it has gone bad.) Okay, let's go!
Just as they're about to leave, Gunther comes out of the back carrying two garbage bags. Larry sees this and stops him.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Hey, buddy! (Flashes his badge.) Are you familiar with Section 11-B of the Health Code that requires all refuse material out the back exit?

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: But then I'd have to go all the way around the dry cleaner place.

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Oh, so you're saying you'd choose convenience over health?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, stop! Larry, okay, can't you just be Larry and not Larry the health inspector guy? Y'know I mean it was really exciting at first but now it's like, okay, so where are we gonna eat ever?

lines.speaker: Larry
lines.text: Well, I suppose I could give him a warning.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thank you. (To Gunther, who's standing there frozen) Okay, go! Go! Go! (He runs off.) (To Larry) Now, if after dinner you still really need to bust someone, I know a hot dog vendor who picks his nose.
Chandler and Joey's, Chandler and Joey are lamenting about how they kicked Ross out.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Maybe, maybe we did a good thing, helping Ross get back on his feet!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes that was a nice place!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Not a lot of closet space, but he can just hang his stuff out the window in a bag!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah!
Pause.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What are we gonna do?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't know. Maybe pizza?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: About Ross!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh! Oh!
The phone rings and Joey answers it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hello! (Listens.) Oh yeah! (To Chandler) It's the apartment manager; Ross put us down as references. (To the apartment manager.) Ross is the greatest guy you'll ever meet! Yeah, he's very reliable.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (grabbing the phone) Of course he has this big huge dog! That uh, barks into the night. (Listens.) Well, who doesn't love dogs? (Thinks.) Ah, he's a tap dancer! (Listens.) Yes, some would say that is a lost art. (Thinks.) He's a pimp! (Listens.) There you go! Yes, he's a pimp. He's a big, tap dancing pimp! (Pause.) Hello?

The apartment manager hung up on him and he hangs up the phone and throws in on the chair. Joey motions, "What the hell was that?" Chandler makes a face to say, "Think about it." Joey tries to divide 136 by 13; he's confused. Suddenly, light dawns on yonder dunder head. He gets it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ohhhhh!
Joey motions, "Now, that's thinking!"

Downstairs at Danny's party, Monica and Rachel are coming down the stairs and Rachel has on a coat to make it look as if she's just getting back. But just as they reach the landing they see Danny out in the hall talking to a guest, Rachel then quickly pulls Monica back up the stairs.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Shoot, shoot, this is never gonna work! He's right there!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Just go over and say hi.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, I have to go downstairs and come back up as if I'm coming home from the regatta gala. Okay? So just go distract him. But don't be sexy.
Monica obeys.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, Danny!

lines.speaker: Danny
lines.text: Hey! What's going on?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (turns his back to the stairs) Oh, it's a great party! Great food. Y'know, most parties it's all chips and salsa, chips and salsa. (As she's saying this Rachel tries to head downstairs but is blocked by people coming upstairs. She quickly retreats back up the stairs.) (Sees that she has to keep him distracted longer.) So umm, what's this? (Points to his plate.)

lines.speaker: Danny
lines.text: Salad.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ooooh! (Rachel now succeeds in getting downstairs.) And-and-and what-what's this? (Points again.)

lines.speaker: Danny
lines.text: Bread. Aren't you a chef?
Rachel returns.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (upon seeing Rachel she points) Oh.

lines.speaker: Danny
lines.text: Hey! Rachel!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey! Oh right, tonight was your party.

lines.speaker: Danny
lines.text: Oh wow, you look great! Glad you could make it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh well, y'know, the gala had to end sometime.

lines.speaker: Danny
lines.text: Don't go anywhere, I'll be right back. (He heads off.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, sure. (To Monica) All right, whose court is the ball in now?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I thought there wasn't a ball?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, come on! He's glad that I came, he doesn't want me to go anywhere, balls flying all over the place!

lines.speaker: Danny
lines.text: (returning, with a friend) Rachel, this is my friend Tom. (To Tom) This is the girl I told you about.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, go on! You telling people about me?

lines.speaker: Danny
lines.text: You two could really hit it off! I'm gonna go mingle. (Leaves.)

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: So you work at Bloomingdale's, huh? My mom calls it Bloomies.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (laughs) Yeah, okay, at ease solider!

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: I'm sorry?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, it's all right, you can just drop the act Tommy. I know what's going on here. Your Danny's wingman right? You guys are best buds. Frat bros!

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: I'm gonna go talk to uh, a friend.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, you go talk to your friend. You tell him, "Nice try."
He walks off.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Man! He just keeps lobbing them up and I just keep knocking them right out of the park!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I think I need a drink.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah!
They go get a drink.
Chandler and Joey's, Ross still has boxes all over the place. Joey is wearing a football helmet, and Chandler is spinning him around in one of the chairs and counting.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (getting up) Dude, I'm telling ya! I'm fine! (He tries to take a step and falls flat on his face.)
He tries to get up again and starts falling backwards and Chandler catches him.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Here we go! Here we go!
Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey. So I uh, I didn't get that apartment. Some problem with my application.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You're kidding!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You're kidding, no!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah. But, the good news is that Phoebe said that I could stay at her place for a while. So...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But you can't stay with Phoebe, Ross! We're-we're roomies!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, you guys don't need me here taking up your space.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, we got plenty of space! There-there's still some over there (Points to where the window is but sees that there isn't any space there and points towards his door.) by-by that speaker. Please, just stay!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Are you guys sure about this?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Definitely!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes! Ross, you have to stay!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right, buddy!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So I'm a pimp huh? It's okay! Look, I know that sometimes I can be a pain in the ass, but you just have to talk to me. Tell me if something is bothering you. Okay? And for my part I will do everything I can to keep my annoying habits just (Does the 'quiet down' maneuver).
Chandler and Joey smile, but when Ross turns away look at each other with looks of horror.
Ending Credits
Chandler and Joey's and Ross's, Chandler is entering and when he closes the door Joey pops his head out of the fort like before, but this time he's wearing a cowboy hat.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I see you've had a very productive day. Don't you think the cowboy hat is a little much?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (popping up behind Joey wearing an Indian headdress) Come on, it's fun!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right! (He joins them in the fort and comes up putting on a bonnet.) Isn't this a woman's hat?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Dude, stop talking crazy and make us some tea!
Chandler does so.
End
